questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a workspace role mentioned in the article?

options.explanation: Workspace owners are listed as a workspace role.
Membership admins are listed as a workspace role.
Teamspace owner is a *teamspace* role, not a workspace role.
Guests are listed as a workspace role.

questions.question: Which sidebar section contains pages that only you can see?

options.explanation: Teamspaces hold shared teamspace pages, not private drafts.
Shared shows pages other people have shared with you.
The Private section is for pages visible only to you.
There is no "All" section in the sidebar.

questions.question: Which teamspace setting allows any workspace member to join the teamspace without an invitation?

options.explanation: Default follows the workspace's default behavior, not necessarily open joining.
Open teamspaces let any workspace member join freely.
Closed teamspaces are visible but require an invitation to join.
Private teamspaces are hidden from non‑members.

questions.question: To make a page viewable by anyone on the internet, which sharing option should you use?

options.explanation: That limits access to only the named people.
Only members of that teamspace would see the page.

options.text: Share with everyone in the workspace
options.explanation: That restricts visibility to workspace members, not the public.
Publishing to the web creates a public link that anyone can view.

questions.question: Which role can control workspace settings and add new members?

options.explanation: Guests have the most limited permissions.
Membership admins are explicitly described as controlling settings and membership.
Members can use the workspace but cannot manage settings.
Teamspace owners manage only their teamspace, not the whole workspace.

questions.question: What does a "Closed" teamspace setting do for workspace members who are not yet members of that teamspace?

options.text: They can see and join automatically
options.explanation: That describes an Open setting.

options.text: They can see the teamspace but need an invitation to join
options.explanation: Closed teamspaces are visible but require an invite to become members.

options.text: They cannot see the teamspace at all
options.explanation: That describes a Private setting.

options.text: They can edit the teamspace but not view it
options.explanation: Impossible – you must view before you can edit.

questions.question: Which permission level allows a user to edit a page?

options.explanation: Full Access includes editing capabilities.
Can View only permits reading, not editing.
The article does not list a separate "Can Comment" level; even if it existed, it wouldn't allow editing.
The article mentions Full Access and Can View, so a level exists for editing.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the "Shared" section in the Notion sidebar?

options.text: To store templates from the gallery
options.explanation: Templates are accessed via the template gallery, not the Shared section.

options.text: To hold pages others have shared with you
options.explanation: Shared contains pages that other people have given you access to.

options.text: To contain listings of all teamspaces
options.explanation: Teamspaces have their own dedicated section.
Private drafts belong in the Private section.

questions.question: A user is a workspace "member" but is not added to a specific teamspace. How does a "Private" teamspace appear to that user?

options.text: They can view the teamspace but not join
options.explanation: Private teamspaces are hidden from non‑members, not just restricted from joining.

options.text: They can join automatically because they are workspace members
options.explanation: Only Open teamspaces allow automatic joining.

options.text: They cannot see the teamspace at all
options.explanation: Private teamspaces are invisible to anyone who isn’t a member or owner of that teamspace.

options.text: They can request access via a button
options.explanation: The article does not mention a request‑access feature for Private teamspaces.

questions.question: If an admin wants every new page to start with the most restrictive permissions possible, which teamspace setting should be used as the default?

options.explanation: Default inherits the workspace’s standard behavior, which may not be the most restrictive.
Open is the least restrictive, allowing anyone to join.
Closed still lets members see the teamspace; it’s not the most restrictive.
Private hides the teamspace from non‑members, providing the highest level of restriction.
